feed.title: Security Advisory for Go modules
feed.subtitle: Security Advisory for Go modules on GitHub

entry.title: [code.gitea.io/gitea] Gitea mishandles authorization for deletion of releases
entry.content:
Gitea before 1.25.2 mishandles authorization for deletion of releases.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-68938
  • https://github.com/go-gitea/gitea/pull/36002/commits/d4262131b39899d9e9ee5caa2635c810d476e43f#diff-8962bac89952027d50fa51f31f59d65bedb4c02bde0265eced5cf256cbed306d
  • https://blog.gitea.com/release-of-1.25.2
  • https://github.com/go-gitea/gitea/releases/tag/v1.25.2
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-cm54-pfmc-xrwx

entry.title: [github.com/mattermost/mattermost-server] Mattermost doesn't verify that post actions invoking `/share-issue-publicly` were created by the Jira plugin
entry.content:
Mattermost versions 11.1.x <= 11.1.0, 11.0.x <= 11.0.5, 10.12.x <= 10.12.3, 10.11.x <= 10.11.7 fail to verify that post actions invoking /share-issue-publicly were created by the Jira plugin which allowed a malicious Mattermost user to exfiltrate Jira tickets when victim users interacted with affected posts

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64641
  • https://mattermost.com/security-updates
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/pull/34551
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/b57c297c6d7ae6812d85e32a625806ac9555deee
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-vww6-79rv-3j4x

entry.title: [github.com/mattermost/mattermost-server] Mattermost doesn't verify that post actions invoking `/share-issue-publicly` were created by the Jira plugin
entry.content:
Mattermost versions 11.1.x <= 11.1.0, 11.0.x <= 11.0.5, 10.12.x <= 10.12.3, 10.11.x <= 10.11.7 fail to verify that post actions invoking /share-issue-publicly were created by the Jira plugin which allowed a malicious Mattermost user to exfiltrate Jira tickets when victim users interacted with affected posts

  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/pull/34551
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/b57c297c6d7ae6812d85e32a625806ac9555deee
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-vww6-79rv-3j4x

entry.title: [github.com/mattermost/mattermost/server/v8] Mattermost doesn't verify that post actions invoking `/share-issue-publicly` were created by the Jira plugin
entry.content:
Mattermost versions 11.1.x <= 11.1.0, 11.0.x <= 11.0.5, 10.12.x <= 10.12.3, 10.11.x <= 10.11.7 fail to verify that post actions invoking /share-issue-publicly were created by the Jira plugin which allowed a malicious Mattermost user to exfiltrate Jira tickets when victim users interacted with affected posts

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64641
  • https://mattermost.com/security-updates
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/pull/34551
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/b57c297c6d7ae6812d85e32a625806ac9555deee
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-vww6-79rv-3j4x

entry.title: [github.com/mattermost/mattermost-server] Mattermost doesn't validate user channel membership when attaching Mattermost posts as comments to Jira issues
entry.content:
Mattermost versions 11.1.x <= 11.1.0, 11.0.x <= 11.0.5, 10.12.x <= 10.12.3, 10.11.x <= 10.11.7 fails to validate user channel membership when attaching Mattermost posts as comments to Jira issues, which allows an authenticated attacker with access to the Jira plugin to read post content and attachments from channels they do not have access to.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-13767
  • https://mattermost.com/security-updates
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/pull/34551
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/b57c297c6d7ae6812d85e32a625806ac9555deee
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-fmqf-pmcm-8cx9

entry.title: [github.com/mattermost/mattermost-server] Mattermost doesn't validate user channel membership when attaching Mattermost posts as comments to Jira issues
entry.content:
Mattermost versions 11.1.x <= 11.1.0, 11.0.x <= 11.0.5, 10.12.x <= 10.12.3, 10.11.x <= 10.11.7 fails to validate user channel membership when attaching Mattermost posts as comments to Jira issues, which allows an authenticated attacker with access to the Jira plugin to read post content and attachments from channels they do not have access to.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-13767
  • https://mattermost.com/security-updates
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/pull/34551
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/b57c297c6d7ae6812d85e32a625806ac9555deee
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-fmqf-pmcm-8cx9

entry.content:
Mattermost versions 11.1.x <= 11.1.0, 11.0.x <= 11.0.5, 10.12.x <= 10.12.3, 10.11.x <= 10.11.7 fails to validate user channel membership when attaching Mattermost posts as comments to Jira issues, which allows an authenticated attacker with access to the Jira plugin to read post content and attachments from channels they do not have access to.

entry.title: [github.com/redhat-developer/gitops-operator] OpenShift GitOps authenticated attackers can obtain cluster root access through forged ArgoCD custom resources
entry.content:
A flaw was found in OpenShift GitOps. Namespace admins can create ArgoCD Custom Resources (CRs) that trick the system into granting them elevated permissions in other namespaces, including privileged namespaces. An authenticated attacker can then use these elevated permissions to create privileged workloads that run on master nodes, effectively giving them root access to the entire cluster.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-13888
  • https://access.redhat.com/errata/RHSA-2025:23203
  • https://access.redhat.com/security/cve/CVE-2025-13888
  • https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2418361
  • https://access.redhat.com/errata/RHSA-2025:23206
  • https://access.redhat.com/errata/RHSA-2025:23207
  • https://github.com/redhat-developer/gitops-operator/pull/897
  • https://github.com/redhat-developer/gitops-operator/commit/bc6ac3e03d7c8b3db5d8f1770c868396a4c2dcef
  • https://github.com/redhat-developer/gitops-operator/releases/tag/v1.16.2
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-pcqx-8qww-7f4v

entry.title: [github.com/kedacore/keda/v2] KEDA has Arbitrary File Read via Insufficient Path Validation in HashiCorp Vault Service Account Credential
entry.content:
Impact

An Arbitrary File Read vulnerability has been identified in KEDA, potentially affecting any KEDA resource that uses TriggerAuthentication to configure HashiCorp Vault authentication.

The vulnerability stems from an incorrect or insufficient path validation when loading the Service Account Token specified in spec.hashiCorpVault.credential.serviceAccount.

An attacker with permissions to create or modify a TriggerAuthentication resource can exfiltrate the content of any file from the node's filesystem (where the KEDA pod resides) by directing the file's content to a server under their control, as part of the Vault authentication request.

The potential impact includes the exfiltration of sensitive system information, such as secrets, keys, or the content of files like /etc/passwd.

Patches

The problem has been patched in v2.17.3 and 2.18.3 as well as in main branch.

Workarounds

The only effective workaround is the strict restriction of permissions for creating and modifying TriggerAuthentication resources within the Kubernetes cluster.

Only trusted and authorized users should have create or update permissions on the TriggerAuthentication resource.

This limits an attacker's ability to configure a malicious TriggerAuthentication with an arbitrary path.

Is my project affected?

If it execute s

kubectl get deploy keda-operator -n keda -o jsonpath="{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}"

and the version is not 2.17.3, 2.18.3 or >= 2.19.0, that version is affected.

References

  • https://github.com/kedacore/keda/security/advisories/GHSA-c4p6-qg4m-9jmr
  • https://github.com/kedacore/keda/commit/15c5677f65f809b9b6b59a52f4cf793db0a510fd
  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-68476
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-c4p6-qg4m-9jmr

entry.title: [github.com/kedacore/keda/v2] KEDA has Arbitrary File Read via Insufficient Path Validation in HashiCorp Vault Service Account Credential
entry.content:
Impact

An Arbitrary File Read vulnerability has been identified in KEDA, potentially affecting any KEDA resource that uses TriggerAuthentication to configure HashiCorp Vault authentication.

The vulnerability stems from an incorrect or insufficient path validation when loading the Service Account Token specified in spec.hashiCorpVault.credential.serviceAccount.

An attacker with permissions to create or modify a TriggerAuthentication resource can exfiltrate the content of any file from the node's filesystem (where the KEDA pod resides) by directing the file's content to a server under their control, as part of the Vault authentication request.

The potential impact includes the exfiltration of sensitive system information, such as secrets, keys, or the content of files like /etc/passwd.

Patches

The problem has been patched in v2.17.3 and 2.18.3 as well as in main branch.

Workarounds

The only effective workaround is the strict restriction of permissions for creating and modifying TriggerAuthentication resources within the Kubernetes cluster.

Only trusted and authorized users should have create or update permissions on the TriggerAuthentication resource.

This limits an attacker's ability to configure a malicious TriggerAuthentication with an arbitrary path.

Is my project affected?

If it execute s

kubectl get deploy keda-operator -n keda -o jsonpath="{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}"

and the version is not 2.17.3, 2.18.3 or >= 2.19.0, that version is affected.

References

  • https://github.com/kedacore/keda/security/advisories/GHSA-c4p6-qg4m-9jmr
  • https://github.com/kedacore/keda/commit/15c5677f65f809b9b6b59a52f4cf793db0a510fd
  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-68476
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-c4p6-qg4m-9jmr

entry.title: [github.com/elastic/beats/v7] Elasticsearch Packetbeat has Excessive Allocation of Memory and CPU via Malicious IPv4 Fragments
entry.content:
Allocation of resources without limits or throttling (CWE-770) allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to cause excessive allocation (CAPEC-130) of memory and CPU via the integration of malicious IPv4 fragments, leading to denial-of-service in Packetbeat.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-68388
  • https://discuss.elastic.co/t/packetbeat-8-19-9-9-1-9-and-9-2-3-security-update-esa-2025-29/384177
  • https://github.com/elastic/beats/commit/28cfc80d2f4e80bfd1c72eb3f849d777751ab870
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-fj69-23m4-ccvv

entry.title: [github.com/elastic/beats] Elasticsearch Packetbeat has Excessive Allocation of Memory and CPU via Malicious IPv4 Fragments
entry.content:
Allocation of resources without limits or throttling (CWE-770) allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to cause excessive allocation (CAPEC-130) of memory and CPU via the integration of malicious IPv4 fragments, leading to denial-of-service in Packetbeat.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-68388
  • https://discuss.elastic.co/t/packetbeat-8-19-9-9-1-9-and-9-2-3-security-update-esa-2025-29/384177
  • https://github.com/elastic/beats/commit/28cfc80d2f4e80bfd1c72eb3f849d777751ab870
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-fj69-23m4-ccvv

entry.title: [github.com/elastic/beats] Elasticsearch Packetbeat has Excessive Allocation of Memory and CPU via Malicious IPv4 Fragments
entry.content:
Allocation of resources without limits or throttling (CWE-770) allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to cause excessive allocation (CAPEC-130) of memory and CPU via the integration of malicious IPv4 fragments, leading to denial-of-service in Packetbeat.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-68388
  • https://discuss.elastic.co/t/packetbeat-8-19-9-9-1-9-and-9-2-3-security-update-esa-2025-29/384177
  • https://github.com/elastic/beats/commit/28cfc80d2f4e80bfd1c72eb3f849d777751ab870
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-fj69-23m4-ccvv

entry.title: [github.com/elastic/beats] Elasticsearch Packetbeat has Excessive Allocation of Memory and CPU via Malicious IPv4 Fragments
entry.content:
Allocation of resources without limits or throttling (CWE-770) allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to cause excessive allocation (CAPEC-130) of memory and CPU via the integration of malicious IPv4 fragments, leading to denial-of-service in Packetbeat.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-68388
  • https://discuss.elastic.co/t/packetbeat-8-19-9-9-1-9-and-9-2-3-security-update-esa-2025-29/384177
  • https://github.com/elastic/beats/commit/28cfc80d2f4e80bfd1c72eb3f849d777751ab870
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-fj69-23m4-ccvv

entry.title: [github.com/mattermost/mattermost-server] Mattermost has an Invite Token Replay Vulnerability via Channel Membership Manipulation
entry.content:
Mattermost versions 10.11.x < 10.11.5, 11.0.x < 11.0.4, 10.12.x < 10.12.2 fail to invalidate remote cluster invite tokens when using the legacy (version 1) protocol or when the confirming party does not provide a refreshed token, which allows an attacker who has obtained an invite token to authenticate as the remote cluster and perform limited actions on shared channels even after the invitation has been legitimately confirmed.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-13324
  • https://mattermost.com/security-updates
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/364c2203de00fe0d8424b6b46d6f0eeb02a2539a
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/7ccb62db7958abd6a4b21a06c5a4f5367a8f8b1f
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/9f54e5cdc3aef412945ff0e6a58338f7b549bdda
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-x3r8-2hmh-89f5

entry.title: [github.com/mattermost/mattermost/server/v8] Mattermost has an Invite Token Replay Vulnerability via Channel Membership Manipulation
entry.content:
Mattermost versions 10.11.x < 10.11.5, 11.0.x < 11.0.4, 10.12.x < 10.12.2 fail to invalidate remote cluster invite tokens when using the legacy (version 1) protocol or when the confirming party does not provide a refreshed token, which allows an attacker who has obtained an invite token to authenticate as the remote cluster and perform limited actions on shared channels even after the invitation has been legitimately confirmed.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-13324
  • https://mattermost.com/security-updates
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/364c2203de00fe0d8424b6b46d6f0eeb02a2539a
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/7ccb62db7958abd6a4b21a06c5a4f5367a8f8b1f
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/9f54e5cdc3aef412945ff0e6a58338f7b549bdda
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-x3r8-2hmh-89f5
